title: Complete Agentic AI Bootcamp With LangGraph and Langchain
headline: Learn to build real-world AI agents, multi-agent workflows, and autonomous apps with LangGraph and LangChain

instructor.name: Krish Naik
instructor.bio: Industry veteran with 13+ years of experience in data analytics. Over 1M+ students worldwide across platforms. Runs KrishNaik Academy.

description: This bootcamp teaches intelligent agent development using LangGraph and LangChain frameworks. Students learn how AI agents differ from traditional models, covering essential components like memory, tools, and decision-making systems. The curriculum spans from foundational AI agent concepts through deploying production-ready multi-agent systems capable of autonomous reasoning and tool integration.
what_youll_learn: Comprehend foundational Agentic AI principles and autonomous agent design for practical applications
Develop proficiency in LangGraph for constructing workflows, handling state, memory, and event-driven systems
Create and implement collaborative multi-agent frameworks capable of communication and complex problem-solving
Core concepts behind Agentic AI and how intelligent agents operate
Hands-on mastery of LangGraph and LangChain for building agent systems
Building autonomous event-driven AI workflows with memory, reasoning, and tools
Deploying and optimizing single-agent and multi-agent applications
Real-world project experience with RAG agents and auto-research agents
prerequisites: Basic knowledge of Python programming (variables, functions, classes)
Understanding of APIs and RESTful services (basic level)
Familiarity with Large Language Model concepts (helpful but not required)
curriculum_sections: Fundamentals of Agentic AI — how agents differ from traditional AI models; key components: memory, tools, decision-making
LangGraph and LangChain Mastery — hands-on experience building and deploying intelligent agents; complex agentic workflows with memory, reasoning, and tools
Single and Multi-Agent Systems — building single-agent and multi-agent collaborative systems that communicate, reason, and solve complex problems
Real-World Applications — autonomous research agents, task automation systems, RAG-based assistants
Environment Setup — Anaconda, VS Code installation and configuration
LangChain and LCEL Fundamentals
Agent Fundamentals, Memory Management, and Tool Usage
Multi-Agent Collaboration Architectures
RAG Agent Implementation
API Deployment with Langserve
target_audience: Software developers and engineers
ML/AI professionals
Data scientists and researchers
Product builders and entrepreneurs
AI enthusiasts and early adopters

reviews.quote: If you're comfortable with Python but new to LangChain, this bootcamp provides the perfect on-ramp and assumes no prior LLM knowledge.

The agent I built in this course handles customer support queries autonomously, reducing response time by 80%. The multi-agent system project teaches how to coordinate multiple AI agents working together.
Power-packed Udemy course on Agentic AI and LLM — the perfect course if you want to master LLMs and AI Agents in tandem.

title: Ultimate RAG Bootcamp Using Langchain, LangGraph & Langsmith
headline: Build powerful RAG pipelines: Traditional, Advanced, Multimodal & Agentic AI with LangChain, LangGraph and Langsmith

instructor.name: Krish Naik
instructor.bio: Industry veteran with 13+ years of experience in data analytics. Over 1M+ students worldwide across platforms. Runs KrishNaik Academy.

description: The Ultimate RAG Bootcamp is a complete, step-by-step guide to mastering RAG using LangChain, LangGraph, and LangSmith. Takes learners from the fundamentals of RAG pipelines all the way to advanced Agentic RAG architectures used in production by leading companies. Covers vector databases, chunking strategies, and production deployment while mastering LangChain, LangGraph, and LangSmith tools.
what_youll_learn: Build traditional RAG pipelines for accurate and efficient information retrieval
Implement advanced retrieval methods like hybrid search, multimodal RAG, and persistent memory
Design multi-agent and autonomous RAG systems using LangGraph for collaborative AI reasoning
Use LangSmith for tracking, debugging, and optimizing RAG workflows in real-world projects
Use vector databases like FAISS, Pinecone, and Weaviate efficiently
Build domain-specific knowledge chatbots with hybrid search
Develop multimodal AI assistants that process both text and images
Take AI apps from development to production with cloud deployment
prerequisites: Basic Python programming knowledge (variables, loops, functions)
Basic LangChain knowledge helpful but not required
curriculum_sections: LangChain — Build modular RAG pipelines
LangGraph — Create advanced agent-based workflows with memory
LangSmith — Track, debug, and evaluate RAG systems
Vector Databases — FAISS, Pinecone, Weaviate, and more
Cloud Deployment — Take AI apps from development to production
Traditional RAG Pipelines
Advanced Retrieval Methods (hybrid search, multimodal)
Multi-Agent Autonomous RAG Systems
Domain-Specific Knowledge Chatbots
Multimodal AI Assistants
target_audience: AI beginners wanting to learn RAG from scratch
Experienced developers looking to master advanced RAG architectures
Data scientists and ML engineers
Anyone interested in production-grade RAG systems

reviews.quote: Bestseller course with aggregate rating of 4.6 out of 5. Strong enrollment base demonstrating course popularity and trust.
So here you go — the Ultimate RAG Bootcamp. Takes you from fundamentals all the way to advanced Agentic RAG architectures.

title: Agentic AI - Private Agentic RAG with LangGraph and Ollama
headline: LangGraph v1, Ollama, Agentic RAG, Private RAG, Corrective RAG, CRAG, Reflexion, Self-RAG, Adaptive RAG, MySQL Agent

instructor.name: Laxmi Kant Tiwari
instructor.bio: IIT Kharagpur graduate with 10+ years of industry experience across startups and MNCs. Worked on real-world ML, deep learning, LLMs, LangChain, LangGraph, RAG systems, and AI agent workflows. KGP Talkie is his global teaching platform.

level: All Levels (advanced-level content; requires prior LangChain/LangGraph knowledge)
description: Advanced, project-based course teaching developers how to build private, production-ready RAG systems using LangGraph, LangChain, Ollama, ChromaDB, Docling, and Python. All projects run 100% locally with no external API cost and no data leaving your system. Uses real SEC financial filings from Amazon, Google, Apple, and Microsoft as data sources.
what_youll_learn: Build private, production-ready Agentic RAG systems using LangGraph v1 and Ollama
Create custom LLM workflows with LangGraph state machines, nodes, edges, and conditional routing
Implement PageRAG, metadata extraction, PDF processing with Docling, and page-level ingestion
Use ChromaDB, embeddings, metadata filtering, and MMR retrieval for high-accuracy search
Apply BM25+ re-ranking and advanced retrieval pipelines for financial document analysis
Build Agentic RAG: tool calling, reasoning loops, structured outputs, and multi-step workflows
Implement Corrective RAG (CRAG) with document grading, query rewriting, and web search fallback
Create custom Ollama models, Modelfiles, embeddings, and integrate with LangChain
Build Reflexion, Self-RAG and Adaptive RAG systems
Build MySQL Agent for database queries
Install and configure Ollama for private LLM deployment
Work with models: Qwen3, GPT-OSS, Llama 3.2, nomic-embed
Master production-grade private RAG systems running entirely locally
prerequisites: Good LangGraph or LangChain knowledge required
Not suitable for absolute beginners in AI
curriculum_sections: Ollama Installation & Configuration — private LLM deployment
Custom LLMs with Modelfiles
LangGraph State Machines — TypedDict, nodes, edges, conditional routing
Complete RAG System from Scratch
PDF Ingestion using Docling — OCR and table extraction
ChromaDB Document Storage — metadata filtering
Advanced Retrieval Pipelines — BM25+ re-ranking
Agentic RAG — tool calling, reasoning loops, structured outputs
Corrective RAG (CRAG) — document grading, query rewriting, web search fallback
Reflexion & Self-RAG
Adaptive RAG
SQL Agent Integration (MySQL)
Multi-step Workflow Creation with State Management
target_audience: Developers who want strong control over their data and full privacy
Engineers wanting complete end-to-end workflows using local LLMs
AI developers interested in production-ready RAG without API costs

title: Production AI Agents with JavaScript: LangChain & LangGraph
headline: Production-grade AI agents with LangChain.js, LangGraph.js, RAG, Next.js, LangSmith & real JS/TS projects

instructor.name: Sangam Mukherjee
instructor.bio: Senior Engineer (L5) at Target with 8+ years experience in full-stack web development. Previously at Walmart and Oracle. YouTube channel with 77,000+ subscribers teaching Web Development, DevOps, and AI-powered app development.

description: Built from the ground up for JavaScript & TypeScript engineers who want real, shippable agentic systems — not disconnected demos. Build a sequence of end-to-end projects that mirror how modern teams ship AI features: clean TypeScript code, clear APIs, JSON contracts, LangGraph orchestration, RAG, proper vector stores, and real Next.js frontends wired to real agents.
what_youll_learn: Design, build and deploy production-ready AI agents using LangChain.js and LangGraph.js with TypeScript/JavaScript
Develop practical projects: web search agents, documentation chatbots with RAG, and agentic workflows with clean APIs
Apply JSON-first methodology, Zod schema validation, tool calling, and structured outputs for reliable, observable agent systems
Modern AI app architecture: UI -> orchestration -> models -> tools -> storage
Where LangChain.js fits, where LangGraph.js fits, and how they work together
Multi-provider setup: OpenAI, Gemini, Groq via a single provider factory
Choosing models (OpenAI / Gemini / Groq / local) smartly for cost, speed & reliability
Tokens, context windows, cost-aware thinking, sampling knobs (temperature, top_p, max_tokens)
Zod schemas as contracts for every response, with validate -> repair -> fallback strategies
Chunk -> embed -> upsert into a real vector store; ask -> retrieve -> summarize with citations & confidence
Deploying & Observing Agents with LangSmith + LangGraph Cloud
Connecting JS agents to LangSmith for logs, spans, errors, prompts
Deploying a LangGraph graph to LangGraph Cloud with API testing and HITL (approve/deny) flows
prerequisites: Familiarity with Node.js and modern JS tooling
No prior AI/ML experience explicitly required
curriculum_sections: Introduction & Course Structure
Foundational Concepts — AI app architecture, model selection, agent definitions
Technical Setup — TS/Node project config, tsconfig, env patterns, multi-provider setup
LLM Fundamentals in JS — tokens, context windows, sampling parameters
Structured Outputs — Zod schemas, validation/repair/fallback strategies
Tool Calling — calculator, date planner, summarize tools
LangGraph Orchestration — nodes, edges, state management
RAG Implementation — chunk, embed, upsert; vector store; retrieve with citations
Next.js Frontend Integration — real frontends wired to agents
Deployment & Observability — LangSmith logs, LangGraph Cloud, HITL flows
target_audience: Full-stack, backend and Next.js engineers ready to add LangChain.js, LangGraph.js, RAG and tool-calling skills
Developers coming from Python-only AI content who want a JS-native, framework-driven path to shipping agentic apps
JavaScript/TypeScript engineers who want production-grade agentic systems

title: LangGraph Mastery: Build AI Agents, RAG & Multi-Agent System
headline: Build AI agents using LangGraph. Learn ReAct agents, Agentic RAG, multi-agent systems, memory & human-in-the-loop apps

instructor.name: Pratham Chandratre
instructor.bio: 13x AI Internships at organizations like Amazon, IBM, EY, and Infosys. 22+ global certifications across AI, ML, GenAI, Agentic AI, Data Science, and Cloud Computing (6x Microsoft Azure, 12x Oracle Cloud, 2x GitHub, 2x Salesforce).

description: Project-based course teaching building modern agentic AI systems rather than simple prompt-response chains. Topics include agent design, multi-agent collaboration, retrieval-augmented generation, memory implementation, human-in-the-loop workflows, and tool integration. Each major section includes a hands-on mini project.
what_youll_learn: Develop production-ready AI agents using LangGraph's node, edge, and state management systems
Design ReAct agents capable of reasoning, executing actions, and leveraging external tools for autonomous problem-solving
Create memory systems enabling agents to maintain conversation history and contextual awareness
Build Agentic RAG systems where AI decides when to retrieve information
Develop multi-agent systems where specialized AI agents collaborate
Build human-in-the-loop AI systems
Design intelligent AI workflows using LangGraph loops, routing, and conditional logic
Integrate tools, APIs, and search systems into AI agents
prerequisites: Basic Python knowledge and curiosity about modern AI technologies
Familiarity with Python libraries (pip, virtual environments) helpful but not mandatory
No prior experience with AI agents or RAG systems needed
curriculum_sections: LangGraph Fundamentals — nodes, edges, state management, intelligent AI workflows
ReAct Agents — reasoning, action execution, external tool usage
Memory Systems — conversation history, contextual awareness
Agentic RAG — AI-driven retrieval decisions
Multi-Agent Systems — specialized agent collaboration
Human-in-the-Loop — interactive AI systems with human oversight
Tool Integration — APIs, search systems, external tools
Mini Projects — hands-on implementation per section
target_audience: Python developers who want to learn how to build AI agents using LangGraph
Machine learning and AI enthusiasts interested in building modern agentic AI systems
Developers who want to move beyond prompt engineering
Engineers and software developers exploring Generative AI
Students and professionals who want hands-on experience building AI agents and agentic workflows
